scope: Pass B full-surface expansion against the real Everhour API reference (https://everhour.docs.apiary.io/). 11 read streams (5 retained from wave2 plus sections, time_off_types, allocations, expense_categories, expenses, invoices) and 17 write actions covering client/project/task/section/time-record/expense lifecycle mutations. Excluded: single-resource detail GETs that duplicate an already-covered list stream's record shape; custom-field, timer, timecard, timesheet, resource-planner-assignment, dashboard-report, and attachment/binary-upload surfaces (requires_elevated_scope/binary_payload/out_of_scope breadth-vs-cost triage -- each has its own specific reason, not a shared boilerplate string); webhooks (no list/discovery endpoint exists, only get/create/update/delete-by-id); and destructive/irreversible or compound multi-step account-level actions (invoice status transitions, invoice line-item resets, accounting-system export, attachment binary payloads).

excluded.reason: single-client detail; identical record shape to the clients list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
client-level budget/period sub-object configuration, a narrow billing-admin action distinct from the core client CRUD triad already covered
client-level budget removal, narrow billing-admin action paired with the excluded budget-set endpoint above

compound invoice-generation action that computes billable line items from a date window and project set at creation time; the invoices stream covers the resulting record shape read-only, generation itself is out of scope
single-project detail; identical record shape to the projects list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
project-level billing-rate/budget sub-object configuration, a narrow billing-admin action distinct from the core project CRUD triad already covered

re-links an external integration's project (GitHub/Trello/Asana/etc) into Everhour by external id; an integration-configuration action, not a plain data-object mutation, and requires an external-system id this connector has no other way to discover
single-section detail; identical record shape to the sections stream's per-item object (per-project fan_out), already covered by that stream
section rename/reposition; a narrow follow-up mutation beyond the core create/delete pair already covered for this sub-resource
single-task detail; identical record shape to the tasks stream's per-item object (per-project fan_out), already covered by that stream
admin-only billing-augmented task detail; a narrower-access variant of the already-covered task detail, not a distinct resource
free-text search over the same task records the tasks stream already lists in full; not a distinct resource shape, a query convenience over already-covered data
project-scoped variant of the same free-text task search; not a distinct resource shape
task time-estimate sub-object set; a narrow follow-up mutation beyond the core task CRUD triad already covered
task time-estimate sub-object removal, paired with the excluded estimate-set endpoint above
project custom-field DEFINITION list (schema metadata, not task data); a narrow admin surface distinct from the core resources this pass prioritizes
custom-field definition creation; narrow admin surface paired with the excluded field-list endpoint above
custom-field definition update; narrow admin surface
custom-field definition delete; narrow admin surface
custom-field display-order reshuffle; cosmetic admin action with no data-object identity of its own
admin-only billing-augmented time-record variant of the already-covered team/time stream, not a distinct resource
per-user filtered view of the same time-record shape team/time already lists in full; not a distinct resource
per-task filtered view of the same time-record shape team/time already lists in full; not a distinct resource
per-project filtered view of the same time-record shape team/time already lists in full; not a distinct resource
starts a live wall-clock timer on a task; a real-time UI-driven action with no meaningful role in a batch sync/reverse-ETL connector
current-user's running timer state; ephemeral live-session data, not a syncable historical record
team-wide running-timer snapshot; ephemeral live-session data, not a syncable historical record
stops the current live timer; a real-time UI-driven action out of scope for a batch connector
single-day clock-in/out timecard detail; part of the timecard/attendance surface excluded this pass for breadth-vs-cost triage
per-user timecard list; part of the timecard/attendance surface excluded this pass for breadth-vs-cost triage
team-wide timecard list; part of the timecard/attendance surface excluded this pass for breadth-vs-cost triage
real-time clock-in action; a live attendance event, not a batch data mutation, part of the excluded timecard surface
real-time clock-out action; paired with the excluded clock-in endpoint above
timecard edit; part of the excluded timecard/attendance surface
timecard delete; part of the excluded timecard/attendance surface
per-user weekly timesheet-approval-state list; part of the timesheet-approval workflow surface excluded this pass for breadth-vs-cost triage
team-wide weekly timesheet list; part of the excluded timesheet-approval workflow surface
submits a timesheet for manager approval; a workflow-state transition, not a plain data-object mutation
discards a pending timesheet-approval request; paired with the excluded submit-for-approval endpoint above
approves or rejects a submitted timesheet; a workflow-state transition with real payroll/billing consequences, out of scope for this pass

team scheduling/resource-planner assignment list; a distinct scheduling-domain surface excluded this pass for breadth-vs-cost triage (time-off allocations and time-off types are covered read-only instead)
creates a scheduling assignment or a time-off request; compound request shape (assignment vs time-off share one endpoint keyed by payload shape) excluded alongside the assignment-list read above
updates a scheduling assignment/time-off request; part of the excluded resource-planner-assignment surface
deletes a scheduling assignment/time-off request; part of the excluded resource-planner-assignment surface
time-off type definition creation; a narrow admin-taxonomy action, the time_off_types stream already covers the resulting record read-only
time-off type definition update; narrow admin-taxonomy action paired with the excluded create endpoint above
time-off type definition delete; narrow admin-taxonomy action

time-off allocation creation; the allocations stream already covers the resulting record shape read-only, and allocation creation carries HR/leave-balance consequences this pass does not extend writes to
time-off allocation update; paired with the excluded create endpoint above
time-off allocation delete; paired with the excluded create endpoint above
single-invoice detail; identical record shape to the invoices list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
duplicate listing of the client-scoped invoice-creation endpoint above; see the /clients/{client_id}/invoices entry
invoice line-item/metadata update; a financial-document mutation with real billing consequences, out of scope for this pass
recomputes an invoice's billable line items from a date window; a compound financial-recalculation action, not a plain data mutation
transitions an invoice to draft/sent/paid; an irreversible financial-workflow state change (marking paid especially) with real accounting consequences

exports an invoice to a connected external accounting system; an integration side-effect, not a plain data mutation, and depends on an external accounting-system connection this bundle has no visibility into
permanently deletes a financial invoice document; irreversible accounting-record destruction excluded from this pass's write scope
expense record update; the core create/delete pair is covered, update is a narrower follow-up mutation excluded this pass for breadth-vs-cost triage
expense-category taxonomy creation; a narrow admin-taxonomy action, the expense_categories stream already covers the resulting record read-only
expense-category taxonomy update; narrow admin-taxonomy action
expense-category delete, which requires a target-category re-mapping body when expenses still reference it; a compound admin-taxonomy action, not a plain data mutation
creates a standalone base64-encoded file attachment (jpg/png/pdf); this dialect's write bodies are JSON/form field maps with no multipart/binary-payload construction mechanism
attaches a base64-encoded file to an expense; same binary-payload construction gap as the standalone attachment-create endpoint above
downloads raw file bytes (image/pdf), not a JSON record; this dialect's read path decodes JSON response bodies only
deletes a file attachment; paired with the excluded binary-payload attachment-creation endpoints above, no attachment stream exists to read one back
computed cross-project reporting rollup, not a syncable object collection; part of the reports surface excluded this pass for breadth-vs-cost triage
computed cross-client reporting rollup, not a syncable object collection; part of the excluded reports surface
computed cross-user/team reporting rollup, not a syncable object collection; part of the excluded reports surface
Everhour's own docs mark this endpoint deprecated
Everhour's own docs mark this endpoint deprecated; the non-deprecated team/time stream already covers the same underlying time-record data
authenticated-caller profile introspection, not a syncable object stream; the check request already confirms auth/connectivity via team/users
no list endpoint exists to enumerate webhook ids; a lone detail-by-id GET with no discovery path is not a syncable stream without an id already in hand from an external source

registers an outbound event-delivery URL of the caller's choosing; webhook management is excluded this pass alongside the list-less GET above (no way to read back and reconcile which webhooks already exist without the missing list endpoint)
updates a webhook's target URL/event set; paired with the excluded webhook-create endpoint above
deletes a webhook; paired with the excluded webhook-create endpoint above
